songs.subject: (chatgpt complex)
songs.lyrics:
It started like a Tuesday,
pale sky, traffic talking too loud.
I had my hands in my jacket,
trying not to need anyone around.

I was doing that thing I do,
half in my head, half gone.
Counting every exit
like the room might do me wrong.

Then you said something simple,
nothing movie-screen bright.
But my shoulders gave up fighting
for the first time that night.

No proof, no history,
no reason I should move closer.
But some old part of me
recognized you before I chose to.

You smiled like we'd met
in a softer version of life,
like I had once been easy
and you remembered me right.

I don't know why I trusted you,
I don't know what my heart knew.
I just stood there by the fire
like the cold had led me to you.

We talked about our names,
old streets, songs we overplay.
Small things on the surface,
but I kept falling through each phrase.

You paused before your answers,
like you cared where they would land.
Looked straight at me without making
a warning out of your hands.

And I got scared of wanting
to know the rooms inside your head.
What makes you laugh too loudly,
what you never say instead.

I thought trust was brick and mortar,
something slow, something earned.
But you came down like weather
and every locked thing turned.

You smiled like we'd met
in a softer version of life,
like I had once been open
and you remembered me right.

I don't know why I trusted you,
I don't know what my heart knew.
I just stood there by the fire
like the cold had led me to you.

Maybe the start wasn't meeting.
Maybe the start was after-
when we stayed past the first light,
past the easy little answers.

When you learned my quiet,
and I learned your fear.
When safe stopped being sudden
and became a place we reappeared.

You smiled like we'd met
in a softer version of life.
Now I'm less afraid of needing,
less rehearsed in saying "I'm fine."

I still don't fully understand it,
but I don't need the whole truth.
Some hearts don't wait for the story-
they just know when they're home too.

It started like a Tuesday,
nothing written in the sky.
Just you, and my guarded body
forgetting how to hide.

songs.subject: (chatgpt complex)
songs.lyrics:
At the edge of the brass-colored sea,
there's a tower with a cracked old bell,
and a man who used to frighten kings
now fixes kettles well.

He taught black ravens tax law once,
made mirrors open wide,
now he loses fights with a broomstick
that says it's retired.

And maybe the thunder forgot his name,
maybe the stars went thin,
maybe the grand words left his mouth
and never came back in.

But there's a blue flame crying in a jar,
there's a lost spell looking for a star,
and you don't need lightning in your hands
to lead one small light home.
No, you don't need the sky to know your name,
don't need the sea to rise in flames,
sometimes the strongest magic says,
"Go home."

Mara came with moonlight
hidden underneath her coat,
said, "It fell out of the dark somehow,"
with fear caught in her throat.

Odrin held it close enough
to hear the silver ring,
not a fire, not a lantern,
but a spell remembering.

The broom laughed low from the corner dust,
"He can't match his socks by noon,"
but Odrin found his weathered cloak
and walked toward the moon.

There's a blue flame crying in a jar,
there's a lost spell looking for a star,
and you don't need lightning in your hands
to lead one small light home.
No, you don't need the sky to know your name,
don't need the sea to rise in flames,
sometimes the strongest magic says,
"Go home."

They climbed the stairs you cannot see
when you stare too hard at things,
past paper gulls and sleeping clouds
with thunder in their wings.

At the top, the night was hanging low,
torn like an old black sheet,
and Odrin raised his useless staff
with nothing grand to speak.

So he didn't split the heavens.
He didn't shake the stones.
He held out both his empty hands
and whispered, "Go home."

Then the blue flame opened like a star,
sewed its bright thread through the dark,
and every compass turned around
to point the sailors home.

Old wells sang low in the yards again,
cupboard charms woke up like friends,
and the broom swept one clean circle
like it meant to all along.

Mara said, "Are you great again?"
He watched the new star burn.
"No," he said, "but useful, maybe."
And that was magic learned.

songs.subject: (chatgpt complex)
songs.lyrics:
Block had weather, you could feel it in the street,
Sun in the laughter, thunder underneath.
Chains look heavy when they shining on the scene,
But looking untouchable ain't the same as being free.

Block had weather, you could hear it in the walls,
Corner store jokes, then the quiet when it falls.
Everybody watching who you grow up tryna be-
But looking untouchable ain't the same as being free.

Malik learned young how the pavement changed pressure,
Kids on bikes swerving like the summer last forever.
Music hit the brick, made the whole block glitter,
Then the air got tight by the sign at Ray's Liquor.

Older boys posted, clean soles, cold chains,
Laughing real loud like they had no real pain.
One nod from Darnell put a crown on his name,
First time he felt seen, first time he felt flame.

At home, fridge humming louder than the talking,
Bills on the table like doors with no knocking.
Mama worked nights, little bro needed shoes,
So "watch the corner" sounded close enough to truth.

Just stand there. Just look. Don't touch. Don't ask.
Cash in his palm made the broke days pass.
Told himself it wasn't deep, just a favor, just a shift-
But the block don't give gifts, it just teaches you the grip.

Block had weather, you could feel it in the street,
Sun in the laughter, thunder underneath.
Chains look heavy when they shining on the scene,
But looking untouchable ain't the same as being free.

For a while it felt beautiful, family in the ride,
Food in the bag, little armor in his stride.
People stepped aside, girls looked twice,
Pocket got weight, but it came with a price.

J-Rock used to joke till the whole curb cracked,
Then cried by the dumpster with his face turned back.
Cousin got hit, now the punchlines broke,
Funny how a loud man can drown in his throat.

Darnell stopped sleeping, jumping out his skin,
Every siren sounded like the walls closing in.
Kids younger than Malik practiced hard-face lessons,
Mirror full of boys turning fear into expression.

Then his little brother asked, eyes wide as a screen,
"Are you famous on the block?" like fame was a dream.
Malik saw himself in the worship and froze-
Cold in his chest where the warning light glowed.

What's a crown if it's rented by fear?
What's a name if you vanish next year?
What's respect when it comes with a debt?
What's a king to a kid who ain't slept?

Next morning, Ray's sign buzzing in the gray,
Darnell called his name, but he walked the other way.
Legs shook bad, pride burned raw,
Every step sounded like it broke some law.

Bills still there. Fridge still sang.
Block still knew everybody by their pain.
Nothing turned gold, no movie-scene escape,
Just a boy choosing breath over weight.

And maybe freedom ain't a door flying open,
Maybe it's your hand letting go of the token.
Maybe it's the walk when your knees feel weak,
Past the same old corner, down the same old street.

He didn't get wings. He didn't get peace.
But he kept one kid from worshiping the leash.
Block had weather, but he finally could see:
Looking untouchable ain't the same as being free.

Block had weather, you could feel it in the street,
Sun in the laughter, thunder underneath.
Chains look heavy when they shining on the scene,
But looking untouchable ain't the same as being free.

Block had weather, but he walked through the rain,
Not 'cause it got easy, not 'cause nothing stayed the same.
Everybody watching who you grow up tryna be-
Looking untouchable ain't the same as being free.

songs.subject: (chatgpt complex)
songs.lyrics:
Every morning Mara wakes
With the city half asleep,
Blue can swinging in her hand,
Golden drops for roots to drink.

Basil bright as emerald glass,
Fern curls soft against the rail,
Tomato climbing toward the sun,
Monstera spreading like a sail.

She checks the soil with careful hands,
Lets the thirsty ones be known,
Some need rain and some need time,
Some are growing on their own.

Pour a little light, pour a little water,
Give the quiet things a chance to bloom.
Turn them toward the morning,
Make a little room.
What you love will answer
In a language soft and true.
Mara said, "You did all this,"
And the leaves said, "So did you."

Sunlight spilled across the leaves
Like honey over green,
Tiny hands reached for the glow,
Making food from air unseen.

Underneath the darkened soil,
Roots were learning how to hold,
Building stems and secret buds
From the warmth and faintest gold.

Day by day the balcony
Grew wild against the gray,
A little jungle in the sky
Where the bees came out to play.

Pour a little light, pour a little water,
Give the quiet things a chance to bloom.
Turn them toward the morning,
Make a little room.
What you love will answer
In a language soft and true.
Mara said, "You did all this,"
And the leaves said, "So did you."

The basil gave its fragrant green,
The fern fell like a waterfall,
The tomatoes wore their yellow stars,
The monstera stood proud and tall.

And maybe care is sunlight,
Maybe patience is the rain,
Maybe growing something gentle
Means you grow a little too again.

Pour a little light, pour a little water,
Give the quiet things a chance to bloom.
Turn them toward the morning,
Make a little room.
What you love will answer
In a language soft and true.
Mara said, "You did all this,"
And the leaves said, "So did you."

In the shimmer, in the shade,
In the breeze that wandered through,
Mara smiled at all they'd made-
The leaves whispered,
"So did you."

songs.subject: (chatgpt complex)
songs.lyrics:
I walked six blocks for a small brass hinge
with your box inside my head
your letters sleeping crooked
under a lid that never sits
Bellman's smelled like rubber boots
and sawdust in the rain
I had the size on a soft white scrap
with my thumb across your name

Aisle seven said FASTENERS
like it knew what I had lost
silver hooks like question marks
black hinges in a box
I moved the little drawers around
like prayer beads, row by row
but the brass was just an empty space
where something used to go

Theo said, "We had them last week"
like last week was a country
I could reach if I left now
he offered silver, offered larger
offered time I didn't have
and the rain kept writing rivers
down the glass

The box still opens if I lift it slow
some things stay broken and still hold
I went for brass and came back with rain
and a paper turning soft around your name
maybe wanting isn't finding
maybe fixing isn't home
the box still opens if I lift it slow

I bought nothing from the counter
nothing folded in a bag
just the bell above the doorway
and the weather at my back
At first I felt foolish
then angry at the sky
then quiet as a kettle
before it starts to cry

I laid the scrap beside the box
like a note I meant to answer
put the water on for tea
your letters didn't ask me
why the lid still leaned away
they just waited there
as patient as could be

The box still opens if I lift it slow
some things stay broken and still hold
I went for brass and came back with rain
and a paper turning soft around your name
maybe wanting isn't finding
maybe fixing isn't home
the box still opens if I lift it slow

A hinge is just a little bridge
between leaving and staying
between the hand that shuts a thing
and the thing that keeps on saying
not yet, not now, not everything
has to close to be complete
some ghosts live in the letters
some live in the creak

The box still opens if I lift it slow
some things stay broken and still hold
I went for brass and came back with rain
and a paper turning soft around your name
maybe wanting isn't finding
maybe missing isn't gone
maybe love is what still opens
when the little hinge is wrong
the box still opens if I lift it slow

songs.lyrics:
I was walking through the noise,
Just another ordinary day,
Keeping all my walls up high,
Letting no one find a way.

Then you looked at me like sunlight,
Like you'd known me all along,
And something in my guarded heart
Started beating like a song.

I didn't have a reason,
Didn't have a clue,
But every quiet part of me
Was leaning into you.

Before I knew your name,
I already felt the flame,
Like my heart had found a place
It had been waiting for.

Before I knew your story,
I trusted you with mine,
Like some souls arrive as strangers
But feel familiar over time.

We talked about the little things,
Music, dreams, and where we'd been,
Every word another doorway
I was slowly stepping in.

I learned the way you smiled
When you tried to hide your fears,
You learned the parts of me
I'd kept untouched for years.

Maybe it was reckless,
Maybe it was true,
But I felt safe for no reason
Standing next to you.

Before I knew your name,
I already felt the flame,
Like my heart had found a place
It had been waiting for.

Before I knew your story,
I trusted you with mine,
Like some souls arrive as strangers
But feel familiar over time.

And I know trust should take forever,
Brick by brick and day by day,
But something in you felt like shelter,
Something made me want to stay.

Now we're more than first impressions,
More than chance beneath the sky,
You're the question I keep asking,
You're the reason I know why.

Before I knew your name,
I already felt the flame,
Like my heart had found a home
Behind an open door.

Before I knew your story,
I trusted you with mine,
Some souls arrive as strangers,
Then feel familiar over time.

I met you as a stranger,
But my heart already knew,
The moment I found safety
Was the moment I found you.

songs.subject: (chatgpt medium)
songs.lyrics:
I walked six blocks with a paper in my hand,
A little brass dream and a simple repair plan.
The rain came down like a whisper on the street,
And I kept time with my hopeful feet.

Bellman's door gave a rusty little ring,
Smelled like sawdust, screws, and everything.
I found the aisle where the small things stay,
But the one thing I needed had slipped away.

There was an empty shelf in the rain,
A little missing piece and a quiet kind of pain.
Sometimes you search, sometimes you wait,
Sometimes you're just one day too late.
I went looking for a way to make it whole,
Came back carrying nothing but my soul.

Theo said, "We had them just last week,"
With a sorry little smile and a gentle way to speak.

songs.subject: (chatgpt medium)
songs.lyrics:
At the edge of the brass-colored sea,
Where the gulls wrote names in foam,
There lived an old wizard in a leaning tower,
With a broom that called it home.
He had bent the rain into silver rings,
He had taught the mirrors to sing,
But now he fixed pots for the fishing boats
And forgot he had served a king.

His cloak was patched with chimney smoke,
His boots were salt and sand,
His kettle coughed like a tired old frog
When he warmed it with his hand.
And the townsfolk said, "Old Odrin Bell
Was mighty once, they claim,"
But the stars were dim, and the spells were thin,
And he hid from his own name.

Oh, magic does not always roar,
It does not always flame,
Sometimes it waits in quiet rooms
For someone small to name.

Carry the fire, carry the light,
Carry the crying star through the night.
Over the cliffs where the wild winds roam,
Sing to the spell till the spell comes home.
No crown, no throne, no thunder above,
Only a jar and a word made of love.
Carry the fire, steady and bright,
Carry the lost star back to the night.

Then Mara came with her coat pulled close,
With rain on her tangled hair,
And under her sleeve was a glass-lidded jar
With a blue flame trembling there.
"It fell from the moon," she softly said,
"And I think it's afraid of the sea.
It has been crying since dawn came up.
Can you help it, wizard? Please?"

Old Odrin laughed, but the laugh fell short
When he heard that silver sound,
Like a bell being rung in a dream below
Where the moonbeams nearly drowned.
"That is no candle, child," he said,
"No lantern, spark, or coal.
That is a spell from the ancient sky,
And it has lost its soul."

The broom in the corner shook its straw
And muttered, "What a mess.
He'll trip on wind, forget the words,
And call it great success."
But Odrin took his crooked staff,
And Mara held the flame,
And the tower door blew open wide
When the moon called out his name.

Carry the fire, carry the light,
Carry the crying star through the night.
Over the cliffs where the wild winds roam,
Sing to the spell till the spell comes home.
No crown, no throne, no thunder above,
Only a jar and a word made of love.
Carry the fire, steady and bright,
Carry the lost star back to the night.

They climbed the stairs that the west wind keeps,
The steps no eye can see,
One foot on air and one on hope,
Far over the brass-bright sea.
The paper gulls flew past their heads,
With wings full of folded prayer,
And clouds with sleeping thunder rolled
Like giants through the air.

They passed a door with no house behind,
A lock with no keyhole made,
A fox of smoke in a velvet hat
Who bowed and softly said,
"The sky is torn where the old spells sleep,
And few remember the tune.
But a child with courage, a jar of blue,
May yet repair the moon."

Higher, higher, the wind-stairs curled,
Above the nets, above the world.
Lower, lower, the sea bells rang,
As every sleeping cupboard sang.
Mara whispered, "Are you afraid?"
Odrin answered, "Yes, I am."
"But fear is just a shadow, child,
That proves there is a lamp."

Carry the fire, carry the light,
Carry the crying star through the night.
Over the cliffs where the wild winds roam,
Sing to the spell till the spell comes home.
No crown, no throne, no thunder above,
Only a jar and a word made of love.
Carry the fire, steady and bright,
Carry the lost star back to the night.

At the top of the world the darkness hung
Like a curtain frayed and thin,
With holes where the old stars should have burned
And the cold came wandering in.
Odrin lifted his staff to speak
The grand words of his prime,
The words that once made mountains kneel
And clocks run backward in time.

But the syllables scattered like frightened birds,
The thunder would not obey,
And all of his splendid wizard names
Blew into the dark away.
Mara looked up with the jar in her hands,
Her face by the blue flame shown.
"Maybe," she said, "it does not need power.
Maybe it just needs home."

So Odrin lowered his ancient staff,
And he did not shout or shine.
He spoke as soft as a candle breath,
As warm as summer wine.
No spell of iron, no royal command,
No storm from a wizard's throne.
He opened the jar, he opened his heart,
And whispered, "Go home. Go home."

Carry the fire, carry the light,
Carry the crying star through the night.
Over the cliffs where the wild winds roam,
Sing to the spell till the spell comes home.
No crown, no throne, no thunder above,
Only a jar and a word made of love.
Carry the fire, steady and bright,
Carry the lost star back to the night.

The blue flame rose like a breath set free,
It circled Mara's face,
Then climbed through the tear in the velvet dark
And stitched it back in place.
A star burst open, clear and new,
A silver-blue delight,
And every compass across the sea
Turned once and settled right.

The wells began to sing again,
The cupboards clicked awake,
The fishermen found their lucky knots,
The old bells ceased to ache.
And back in the tower, the stubborn broom
Swept circles on the floor,
Then coughed and said, "I am retired,"
And swept a little more.

Mara asked, "Are you great again?"
The wizard smiled at the sky.
"No, child, greatness is a noisy coat
I no longer care to try.
But useful magic, quiet magic,
The kind that mends what's torn,
May shine much longer than crowns or storms
When a frightened star is born."

So remember the tower by the brass-colored sea,
Remember the jar held tight,
Remember the girl and the faded old mage
Who carried a star through night.
For magic is not always thunder,
And heroes are not always known.
Sometimes the strongest spell in the dark
Is simply: "Go home. Go home."

songs.lyrics:
I was born under streetlights, chasing shadows down the road,
With a pocket full of questions and a heart I couldn't hold.
Every window looked like heaven from the sidewalk where I stood,
Every stranger had a story, every silence understood.

There were sirens in the distance, there were trains beneath the rain,
There were names I kept repeating just to feel them once again.
I learned love from broken letters, learned goodbye from closing doors,
Learned that sometimes what you're missing is not missing anymore.

And I don't know where the years went,
I don't know why the sky bends,
But I know when the night gets heavy,
I can still hear your voice.

So keep the fire burning, keep the windows open wide,
Let the lonely wind come running, let it shake what's left inside.
We are more than all the wreckage, more than every place we've been,
We are songs beneath the silence, we are light beneath the skin.

And when the stars fall over, when the dark is at the door,
When we can't remember what we were fighting for,
Take my hand and don't look back, let the whole world turn to dust,
We will walk until the morning, until the morning finds us.

You were dancing in a kitchen with the radio too loud,
Barefoot on a battlefield, laughing like you'd beat the clouds.
You said, "Everybody's running from the ghosts they never name,
But if you keep your eyes wide open, even ghosts can turn to flame."

There were coffee cups and bad dreams, there were maps we never used,
There were promises like pennies in the bottom of our shoes.
We were rich with almost nothing, we were kings of getting by,
Building castles out of cardboard, throwing prayers into the sky.

And I don't know how the heart heals,
I don't know what the truth feels,
But I know when the world gets quiet,
I still hear your voice.

So keep the fire burning, keep the windows open wide,

And when the stars fall over, when the dark is at the door,
When we can't remember what we were fighting for,
Take my hand and don't look back, let the whole world turn to dust,
We will walk until the morning, until the morning finds us.

I have carried every winter like a stone inside my chest,
I have smiled in crowded rooms and still felt emptier than the rest.
I have called a thousand numbers just to hear the dial tone,
I have sat with all my failures like they paid to share my home.

But there's something in the breaking that the broken recognize,
There's a language in the thunder, there's a mercy in the skies.
There's a doorway in the darkness, there's a key beneath the pain,
There's a version of tomorrow that is calling out your name.

If the river takes the bridge out,
We'll learn to swim across.
If the compass starts to spin now,
We'll be found by getting lost.

If the mountains stand against us,
We'll carve a path through stone.
If the whole world says surrender,
We'll keep singing on our own.

Oh, we've been bent but never buried,
We've been bruised but never done.
We've been hiding from the daylight
When we were born to face the sun.

Breakdown
And I know, I know, I know,
There are nights that feel too long.
And I know, I know, I know,
You get tired of being strong.

But the dawn is not a rumor,
It is waiting on the hill.
Even when the heart is shaking,
It is beating, beating still.

So keep the fire burning, keep the windows open wide,
Let the lonely wind come running, let it shake what's left inside.
We are more than all the wreckage, more than every place we've been,
We are songs beneath the silence, we are light beneath the skin.

And when the stars fall over, when the dark is at the door,
When we can't remember what we were fighting for,
Take my hand and don't look back, let the whole world turn to dust,
We will walk until the morning, until the morning finds us.

Until the morning finds us,
Until the morning calls.
Until the fear behind us
Turns to writing on the walls.

Until the roads remember
Every footstep, every scar.
Until we stop pretending
That we don't know who we are.

Keep the fire burning,
Keep your head above the rain.
Every ending has an echo,
Every loss can learn your name.

And when the night is over,
When the first light turns to gold,
We'll be standing in the quiet,
Still alive and still not old.

Take my hand and don't look back,
Let the whole world turn to dust.
We will walk until the morning,
Until the morning finds us.

songs.subject: moving back to hometown, americana
songs.lyrics:
I crossed the county line at a quarter past ten
With a dashboard full of memories and a road worn thin
The water tower still stood against the summer sky
But the paint was new, and the old name had gone by
The diner where we gathered after Friday night games
Was a pharmacy now with unfamiliar names
I rolled down the window, let the evening settle in
Trying to find the town I carried all these years within

Oh, my hometown, what have the seasons done?
The fields still catch the gold light of the setting sun
But the faces and the storefronts drift like river sand
I came back looking for yesterday's land
And every mile says nothing stays the same
Still I hear my younger self calling my name
In the echoes of the streets I've always known
I'm a stranger and a native in my hometown

The old mill by the creek is just a memory now
A row of houses stands where it once bowed
The church bell rings the same song every Sunday morn
Though half the folks I knew have moved on or are gone
I saw my high school sweetheart at the grocery store
We talked about the years and nothing more
Then she smiled and said, "This place keeps moving on"
And I knew she was right before she was gone

Oh, my hometown, what have the seasons done?
The fields still catch the gold light of the setting sun

Maybe I was changing all along
While I blamed the years and sang the same old songs
Maybe home is not a place that waits in time
Maybe home is every version left behind

Now the courthouse clock glows soft above the square
And a cool wind carries cut grass through the air
I can mourn what disappeared and still belong
To the roads that taught my heart where it was from
So I'll raise a glass to the people and the past
To the moments we believed would always last
And I'll walk these changing streets until the dawn
Knowing both the town and I have traveled on

Of coming back home and finding new ground

songs.subject: winter breakup, indie rock
songs.lyrics:
The radiator hissed like it had something to say
While the daylight disappeared before the end of the day
We were drinking cheap coffee in mismatched mugs
Talking less every night, giving half-hearted shrugs
Your coat hung by the doorway for weeks at a time
But somehow you felt farther than a state line
Snow gathered quietly against the glass
And neither of us asked how long it would last

Nothing shattered, nothing burned
Just a slow fade we never turned

We broke up over one long winter
In a hundred little silences
Like footprints filling up with snow
Until there was nowhere left to go
No final fight, no slamming door
Just less of us than there was before
By the time the spring wind finally blew
There was no me and you

The city wore the same gray sweater every day
And we watched the headlights smear across the freezing rain
You slept facing the wall, I stayed up too late
Pretending every distance had another name
We canceled plans and called it being tired
Stopped sharing dreams, stopped getting inspired
The records kept spinning in the corner of the room
While something invisible kept making more room

Nothing sudden, nothing loud
Just two ghosts inside a crowd

We broke up over one long winter
In a hundred little silences
Like footprints filling up with snow
Until there was nowhere left to go
No final fight, no slamming door
Just less of us than there was before
By the time the spring wind finally blew
There was no me and you

I kept waiting for a moment I could point to
A sentence, a mistake, a crack running through
But it happened in the spaces between words
In every "maybe later" left unheard
And when the ice began to melt away
There was nothing left for us to save

The first warm afternoon arrived without a sound
People filled the parks, flowers pushed from the ground
You packed a box and took it down the stairs
Like you'd been leaving for months without going anywhere
I stood alone beside the open frame
Watching sunlight fall where your shadow used to stay
And for the first time all season long
The quiet finally felt honest instead of wrong

We broke up over one long winter
In a hundred little silences
Like footprints filling up with snow
Until there was nowhere left to go
No final fight, no slamming door
Just less of us than there was before
By the time the spring wind finally blew
There was no me and you

Now every December when the evenings arrive early
I remember how slowly a heart can leave
Not in a flash, not in a storm
Just one long winter changing form
Until the thaw reveals the truth
Of what the cold already knew

songs.subject: two jobs behind on rent, neo-soul
songs.lyrics:
Alarm clock singing at a quarter to five
Moon still hanging while I'm trying to survive
Coffee in a paper cup, dreams on hold
Trading all my daylight for a little payroll
Punch in at the diner, punch out at noon
Change shirts in the restroom, back at it soon
Everybody says hard work will open the door
But I'm giving everything and needing more

Every dollar got a place to go
Leaves my hands before I even know

I'm working two jobs, still falling behind
Running so hard but I'm losing time
Rent's due again and the numbers don't bend
Stretching every paycheck to the bitter end
I keep pouring from an empty cup
Praying somehow it'll be enough
But the bills keep calling, don't care how hard I try
Working two jobs, still falling behind

Smile for the customers, keep the rhythm smooth
Then stock shelves all evening in my worn-out shoes
My body says stop but the schedule says go
Got responsibilities heavier than they know
The landlord's notice sitting by the door
Like a shadow waiting on the floor
I count every penny, every nickel, every dime
Still the math keeps stealing peace of mind

Can't buy rest, can't purchase grace
Just another shift to chase

I'm working two jobs, still falling behind
Running so hard but I'm losing time
Rent's due again and the numbers don't bend
Stretching every paycheck to the bitter end
I keep pouring from an empty cup
Praying somehow it'll be enough
But the bills keep calling, don't care how hard I try
Working two jobs, still falling behind

Some nights I stare out the window at the city lights
Wonder who gets to live without this fight
I'm not asking for diamonds, not reaching for the sky
Just a little room to breathe, a chance to get by
But every step forward feels borrowed from tomorrow
And every little victory gets swallowed by the sorrow

Still I rise
Still I show up
Still I carry all this weight
Even when the road gets rough
Still I rise
Still I believe
There's a better day somewhere waiting on me

Working two jobs, still falling behind

Working two jobs, still falling behind

songs.subject: friend who moved away, folk-pop
songs.lyrics:
You left in late September with a map across your knees
Chasing bigger skylines and a different kind of breeze
We promised we'd stay close, said the distance wouldn't win
For a while your name kept lighting up my phone again
You sent pictures of the mountains, crowded streets at night
I told you all the hometown news and everything was right
It felt like we could stretch a thousand miles thin
And still find our way back to where we'd always been

But the messages got shorter as the months rolled by
From long conversations to a quick reply
Now I scroll through old words like faded photographs
Trying to hold on to something that wasn't built to last
I guess some roads keep going when they disappear from view
And sometimes losing a friend is nobody's fault but true
Still I miss the way we'd talk about everything under the sun
Before you moved across the country and slowly came undone

Then every "I'll call you soon" felt a little more blunt
I learned about your birthdays from pictures online
Saw new faces standing where I thought I'd be in time
Meanwhile I kept your number at the top of my screen
Saving little stories from days you hadn't seen
But every thread we shared began to fray and bend
Like a rope letting go one strand at the end

From long conversations to a quick reply
Now I scroll through old words like faded photographs
Trying to hold on to something that wasn't built to last
I guess some roads keep going when they disappear from view
And sometimes losing a friend is nobody's fault but true
Still I miss the way we'd talk about everything under the sun

So I wish you all the happiness your new world can bring

songs.subject: leaving the church, alt-country
songs.lyrics:
Sunday morning came with ironed shirts and polished shoes
A leather-bound certainty I never got to choose
Mama sang the hymns like they were stitched into her skin
Daddy quoted scripture when the world came closing in
Every question had an answer printed black and red
Every doubt was something dangerous better left unsaid
So I learned to keep my wondering buried out of sight
Like a porch light switched off in the middle of the night

I didn't leave in anger, I didn't leave in flames
I just walked a little farther than the boundary they gave
Past the steeple and the gravel and the roads I always knew
Carrying the good and bad and trying to sort the truth
Now when the bells are ringing, I hear them from afar
And I don't need all the answers to know who you are
I just quietly kept moving when the time had come
And left the church the same way that the daylight leaves the sun

I remember summer revivals under canvas in the heat
The preacher pounding certainty from a wooden folding seat
The fear and fascination tangled tightly in my chest
Wondering why honest questions felt like such a test
There were people there I loved, people kind and true
People doing all they knew to help each other through
But somewhere in the middle of the promises and rules
I started seeing shades of gray they called impossible

I didn't leave in anger, I didn't leave in flames
I just walked a little farther than the boundary they gave
Past the steeple and the gravel and the roads I always knew
Carrying the good and bad and trying to sort the truth
Now when the bells are ringing, I hear them from afar
And I don't need all the answers to know who you are
I just quietly kept moving when the time had come
And left the church the same way that the daylight leaves the sun

Some nights I still remember every word of every song
They rise up from the distance like echoes carried on
And I don't hate the place that taught me how to hope
I just couldn't make my heart fit inside that narrow road
So I traded certainty for the freedom not to know
And found a different kind of peace out on my own

Mama asks if I've been praying when we talk on the phone
I tell her life's been busy and I leave some things alone
Daddy doesn't bring it up, though I can see he knows
There's a sadness in the silence where the conversation goes
But love is bigger than agreement, bigger than a creed
Bigger than the fear of where a different path might lead
So we meet around the table and pass the bread around
Trying to hear each other over old familiar sounds

I didn't leave in anger, I didn't leave in flames
I just walked a little farther than the boundary they gave
Past the steeple and the gravel and the roads I always knew
Carrying the good and bad and trying to sort the truth
Now when the bells are ringing, I hear them from afar
And I don't need all the answers to know who you are
I just quietly kept moving when the time had come
And left the church the same way that the daylight leaves the sun

There's a cross above the highway as I drive through my hometown
It catches evening colors when the sky is settling down
And I nod to all the memories, then continue on my way
Thankful for the road behind and the one I'm on today

songs.subject: flea market hustle, rap
songs.lyrics:
Sun-up, one-up, unfold the canopy quick,
Got a van full of value and a gambler’s grin slick.
I’m stackin’ knickknacks, flip facts, pitch with a twist,
Turn a “maybe, come back” to a “pay me for this.”
Got a crate of old plates and a radio relic,
Make a faded cassette sound collector-prophetic.
I’m a bargain-card shark with a market-made hustle,
Talk spark to a mark, watch the hard sell muscle.
See a lamp with a dent? That’s “vintage character,” chief,
A scratch on a record? “Adds a little analog grief.”
Dense cents from the fence-sitters, inch by an inch,
Got a nickel-turn trick and a wrinkle-free pitch.
I’m wheelin’ while I’m dealin’, keep the feelin’ kinetic,
Price tag, wisecrack, every line’s arithmetic.
By noon I’m balloonin’ the loot from the lootless,
Turn a box of odd socks to a profitable blueprint.

Sunday mornin’, flea-market stormin’,
Cash in the jar while the crowd keeps swarmin’.
Haggle, rattle, make a flat sale soar,
“Five? I can’t do five”—then it walks out the door.
Sunday mornin’, flea-market stormin’,
Sun on the tarp and the deals keep formin’.
Buy low, say “hello,” let the wordplay work,
Every quirky little trinket got a CEO perk.

songs.subject: outgrowing hometown friends, folk
songs.lyrics:
The water tower's rusting where we carved our names,
The diner neon flickers, but it glows the same.
You still meet on Fridays by the baseball field,
Trading stories that the years have never asked to heal.
I come back every winter, and the streets still know
Every shortcut through the silence, every patch of snow.
But the words don't fit as easy as they used to then,
Like a coat I kept for comfort that won't close again.

And it hurts in the quiet, not the way you'd think,
Like watching shorelines fade while you're still in the same blink.
No one did me wrong, no bridges had to burn,
We just kept on moving at a different turn.
I still love the kids we were beneath those porchlight stars,
But the distance isn't measured by the miles in our cars.
It's the slow, soft ache of becoming someone new,
While the town keeps holding on to who it knew.

Sometimes I hear your laughter in the grocery aisle,
Catch a ghost of seventeen that stays a little while.
And I wonder if you miss me, or the version that remained,
Frozen in a photograph before we learned to change.
The church bell rings at sunset, and the valley answers back,
Every memory a lantern on a half-forgotten path.
I could walk it to the end, but I know what I'd find:
Love that never left, and a life I left behind.

songs.subject: diner waitress ex-musician, folk-rock
songs.lyrics:
The coffee pot was hissing in the pale fluorescent dawn,
She tied her apron loosely and the breakfast rush rolled on.
Truckers by the window, local farmers at the bar,
Never guessed she'd chased the moon in a weather-beaten car.
There was a photograph behind the register and till,
Faded like a summer fair poster on a hill.
Four young dreamers grinning in the glow of stage-side light,
Before the miles grew heavy and the wheels outran the night.
She used to sing harmonies above the diesel hum,
From Memphis down to Denver, every town they'd stumble from.
A twelve-string in the backseat and a notebook full of lines,
Living off cheap gasoline and borrowed goodbyes.

Now she pours another refill, says, "Honey, take your time,"
While the jukebox plays a shadow of a life she left behind.
The road keeps rolling somewhere, and the headlights still burn bright,
But she's learned that every freedom has a different kind of price.
And sometimes when the diner empties out and day turns blue,
She can hear the crowd still calling through the clatter and the fumes.

The band was called The River Sons though half of them were girls,
They played for drink tickets and promises and worlds.
Some nights they packed the dance floor, some nights nobody came,
But they carried every silence like another badge of flame.
Then one winter in Dakota, when the snow swallowed the signs,
The drummer talked of settling down, the bass player drew a line.
The singer chased a record deal out west beyond the sea,
And the road that bound them tightly split as quietly as leaves.
She stayed a while in Nashville, sang to rooms of thirty chairs,
Learned how hope can fill a building and still vanish into air.
Then her father took ill back home, and one phone call changed the map,
So she traded endless highways for a counter and a cap.

Now she pours another refill, says, "Honey, take your time,"
While the jukebox plays a shadow of a life she left behind.
The road keeps rolling somewhere, and the headlights still burn bright,
But she's learned that every freedom has a different kind of price.
And sometimes when the diner empties out and day turns blue,
She can hear the crowd still calling through the clatter and the fumes.

One July evening, a traveler with a guitar case in hand
Stopped for pie and coffee while a storm swept through the land.
He mentioned towns she'd played before and clubs that used to stand,
And for a moment all the years unfolded like a fan.
She laughed and named the highways and the songs she used to know,
The cheap motels, the broken strings, the long forgotten shows.
Then she sang a line she'd written when she was twenty-three,
And the whole room fell so silent you could hear the buzzing screens.
The traveler said, "You still got it."
She smiled and shook her head.
"Maybe songs don't leave you,
They just find another bed."

Closing time came slowly as the neon blinked outside,
She stacked the chairs and counted tips beneath the amber light.
The old band photo caught her eye as midnight settled in,
And she swore she heard a harmony rise softly from within.
Not every dream goes missing,
Not every road must end.
Some become the stories
That you carry as a friend.
And somewhere out beyond the dark where freight trains cut the sky,
A song she helped create is still alive and rolling by.

So she pours another refill, says, "Honey, take your time,"
With a little bit of starlight still surviving in her eyes.
The road keeps rolling somewhere, and she's thankful that it did,
For the woman she became and for the reckless hopeful kid.
And when the morning sunlight paints the chrome and checkered floor,
She hums an old tour melody and opens up the door.

songs.subject: lullaby for sleepless partner, acoustic
songs.lyrics:
The house is quiet, the night is slow,
Moonlight resting on the floor below.
You don't have to chase the dark away,
I'm right here beside you, and I'll stay.

Close your eyes, love, let the day drift by,
Lay your worries down beneath the sky.
You don't have to carry every star tonight,
I'll hold the shadows till the morning light.

The wind is singing softly through the trees,
A gentle rhythm moving with the breeze.
If your thoughts keep wandering far from home,
Take my hand, you don't have to roam.

Close your eyes, love, let the day drift by,
Lay your worries down beneath the sky.
You don't have to carry every star tonight,
I'll hold the shadows till the morning light.

And if sleep comes slowly, that's alright,
We'll listen to the silence of the night.
One breath, then another, calm and deep,
I'm here with you until you fall asleep.

Close your eyes, love, let the day drift by,
Lay your worries down beneath the sky.
You don't have to carry every star tonight,
I'll hold the shadows till the morning light.

songs.subject: underestimated battle rap, boom-bap
songs.lyrics:
Been the dark-horse forecast in a short-form portrait,
Written off quick with a half-baked forfeit.
They saw the calm face, never clocked the coordinates,
Now I map circles round your whole thought orbit.
Underrated, updated, built from the basement,
While they all debated, I was laying the pavement.
Made patience a weapon, every slight an investment,
Now the crowd study my moves like exam questions.
Y'all judged books by the spine and the shelf wear,
Missed the fine print, now the lines hit like health care.
Self-made trailblazer, detail curator,
Turned doubt into fuel and the fumes into generators.
I was "maybe" to the gatekeepers, "probably not,"
Now I'm raising the bar while they lower the shot.
Got a catalogue of comebacks, properly stocked,
Every no was a lockpick, every obstacle popped.
Pen game precise, got the metrics and method,
Pressure-tested efforts turned skeptics to freshmen.
You talk big in the present, I got receipts from the past,
Where they laughed at the draft, now they're studying the craft.

Whole life underestimated, now the truth lands heavy,
Built a throne from the stones they threw already.
They said, "Not him," now the boom-bap say different,
Every missed prediction turned the mission more efficient.
Whole life underestimated, that's the theme of the track,
Came from the back of the pack, now I'm leading it back.
They looked past me, that's exactly what I needed to grow,
Now the same closed doors got a front-row show.

songs.subject: empty nest, americana
songs.lyrics:
We watched the taillights fade beyond the county line,
A pickup full of boxes and a future full of time.
Your old room's still a snapshot of the life you used to know,
A baseball glove, some concert stubs, a shelf of paperbacks in rows.
The dog keeps checking hallways when the screen door starts to creak,
Like he still expects your footsteps every afternoon this week.
And the kitchen clock sounds louder than it's ever sounded here,
Ticking through the spaces where your laughter used to steer.

It's a quiet kind of heartache, it's a sweet and lonesome song,
Knowing we did something right by helping you move on.
The house is full of memories, but tonight it's full of air,
And every room reminds us that you're somewhere out there.
We miss you more than words can say, but we're proud of who you'll be,
That's the bittersweet horizon every parent comes to see.

Your mother folds the laundry and still saves your favorite mug,
I find myself retelling old stories just because.
The porch swing keeps on swaying in the amber evening light,
Like time is taking one slow breath and settling for the night.
The years went by like thunderstorms rolling through July,
Science fairs and soccer games and teaching you to drive.
Now the driveway's standing empty under stars we used to share,
And the silence wears your absence like a prayer hanging in the air.

It's a quiet kind of heartache, it's a sweet and lonesome song,
Knowing we did something right by helping you move on.
The house is full of memories, but tonight it's full of air,

songs.subject: paid off debts, funk-soul
songs.lyrics:
Used to wake up every Monday with a knot behind my grin,
Bills lined up like bouncers saying, "You can't come in."
Counting every penny, stretching every dollar thin,
Now the ledger's singing harmony and the good times rolling in.
I made friends with overtime, had coffee for a king,
Turned "maybe next year" into a real and living thing.
Now I strut into the sunshine with a brand-new state of mind,
No collectors on my shoulder, no chains around my time.

I'm debt-free, baby, look at me glide,
Got a little extra rhythm in my step and in my stride.
The weight is off my back, the pressure off my soul,
I'm dancing through the doorway and I'm finally in control.
Debt-free, baby, let the whole world see,
I got my future in my pocket and it's looking good on me.
Raise a glass, spin around, let the horns all testify,
I paid it off, I paid it all, and now I'm feeling fly.

The mailbox ain't a horror show, the phone don't make me flinch,
I can breathe a little deeper every time I get a glimpse
Of that beautiful zero where a balance used to sit,
Like a groove so smooth you can't help but move your hips.
Bought myself a fancy dinner, left a generous tip,
Not because I had to prove it, just because it felt legit.
Every sacrifice and side gig, every late-night second shift,
Turned into this funky freedom and I'm riding every bit.

I'm debt-free, baby, look at me glide,
Got a little extra rhythm in my step and in my stride.
The weight is off my back, the pressure off my soul,
I'm dancing through the doorway and I'm finally in control.

I got my future in my pocket and it's looking good on me.
Raise a glass, spin around, let the horns all testify,
I paid it off, I paid it all, and now I'm feeling fly.

songs.subject: lighthouse keeper ballad, dark-folk
songs.lyrics:
On the black-toothed coast where the cold waves climb,
Stood a lighthouse keeper counting out his time.
The gulls flew low and the fog rolled deep,
And the sea kept secrets it was sworn to keep.
A traveler came when the autumn bled,
With a silver-handled knife and a coat of red.
He asked for fire, he asked for ale,
While the storm beat hard on the window pale.
Three nights passed beneath the rain,
And no soul saw the traveler again.
Just the keeper staring from the lantern room,
As the bell buoy moaned through the gathering gloom.

Oh, the light turns slow where the dark tides crawl,
And the dead don't rest where the breakers fall.
Hear the wind cry out through the salt-stained night,
For the man who vanished in the keeper's light.
Round and round goes the lonely beam,
Sweeping over the sea like a guilty dream.

Winter came with its iron breath,
And whispers drifted in from the west.
They searched the cliffs and the frozen shore,
But the traveler walked this earth no more.
Then one cold dawn when the sea lay still,
A bone-white hand washed under the hill.
And tangled near in the kelp and foam
Was a silver knife that had followed him home.
The townsfolk gathered with lowered eyes,
Beneath the gulls and the lead-gray skies.
The keeper swore by the moon and tide,
But the truth sat heavy where his heart would hide.

Oh, the light turns slow where the dark tides crawl,
And the dead don't rest where the breakers fall.
Hear the wind cry out through the salt-stained night,
For the man who vanished in the keeper's light.
Round and round goes the lonely beam,
Sweeping over the sea like a guilty dream.

Some say the traveler cheated him,
At cards, at gold, at everything.
Some say a woman stood between
Two lonely souls and a wedding ring.
But others claim when the fog rolled in,
The keeper heard a ghostly hymn.
And followed a voice down the rocks below,
To a place no living man should go.
Now on storm-black nights when the waves run wild,
You can hear two footsteps, mile after mile.
One climbs the tower, one roams the sea,
Bound together for eternity.

Oh, the light turns slow where the dark tides crawl,
And the dead don't rest where the breakers fall.
Hear the wind cry out through the salt-stained night,
For the man who vanished in the keeper's light.
Round and round goes the lonely beam,
Sweeping over the sea like a guilty dream.
And the lighthouse keeper still tends the flame,
While the ocean whispers the missing man's name.

songs.subject: community gospel-house
songs.lyrics:
When the morning feels heavy and the road gets long,
There's a hand at your shoulder saying, "Keep moving on."
When the rain starts falling and the lights burn low,
There's a circle of love that won't let you go.
We bring the chairs out, we bring the food,
We bring our laughter and a hopeful mood.
Every heart's a candle when it's shining bright,
And together we can light up the night.

Show up, show love, that's the way we rise,
Lift each other higher, put the hope back in our eyes.
Side by side, we carry what we can,
A thousand little kindnesses make a stronger stand.
Oh, we're stronger together, hear the voices sing,
When one of us is hurting, everybody brings
A little more grace, a little more light,
We're a house built on love and we're holding on tight.

When a neighbor needs help, we don't look away,
We bring what we've got and we show up that day.
A meal on the table, a ride across town,
A reminder that nobody's got to struggle alone now.
Every story matters, every soul belongs,
Different roads, different rhythms, one song.
From the front porch conversations to the crowded dance floor,
The more we give away, the more we find there's more.

Show up, show love, that's the way we rise,
Lift each other higher, put the hope back in our eyes.
Side by side, we carry what we can,
A thousand little kindnesses make a stronger stand.
Oh, we're stronger together, hear the voices sing,
When one of us is hurting, everybody brings
A little more grace, a little more light,
We're a house built on love and we're holding on tight.

Hands up, hearts open wide,
Nobody left standing on the outside.
One beat, one family,
Moving in the rhythm of solidarity.
And when the world says "divide,"
We answer with an open door.
When the world says "you're on your own,"
We answer, "No more."

Show up, show love, that's the way we rise,
Lift each other higher, put the hope back in our eyes.
Side by side, we carry what we can,
A thousand little kindnesses make a stronger stand.

We're a house built on love and we're holding on tight.

songs.subject: broke but happy, jazz-rap
songs.lyrics:
"How you doin'?" Funny story, kinda glorious, actually,
Wallet lookin' anorexic, but my laugh is eating lavishly.
Got two bucks and loose change, true, strange little treasury,
Yet I'm strolling with a pep-step rhythm and a recipe.
Rent's paid late? Well, lately I've been making peace with maybes,
Trading fancy for a park bench symphony of babies
Laughing at pigeons. Listen—life's got hidden dividends,
If joy's a stock, I'm oddly rich despite the incidentals, friend.
My fridge got a lonely jar and half a lime reclining,
But the sunset on the fire escape? Impeccable timing.
The bank app sends notifications full of mild concern,
I send a nod back like, "Appreciate it, we'll adjourn."
No yacht, no watch that costs a small suburban zip code,
But I got folks who split a pizza four ways and still joke.
Conversation got flavor, improvisation got swing,
When the brass hits a backbeat, man, I swear I got everything.

I'm broke, no joke, but I'm floating just fine,
Got a pocket full of rhythm and a head full of shine.
Ain't counting big money, I'm counting good days,
Little wins, big grins, in a hundred small ways.
I'm broke, no joke, but I'm rich where it counts,
Got friends at the table and a laugh that amounts
To more than a number in a ledger can say—
Got less in the bank, but more life in the day.

My budget does yoga—lots of stretching involved,
Every problem says "complicated," I say "to be solved."
Coffee shop philosopher with a thrift-store blazer,
Looking underfunded, feeling over-favored.
The landlord wants figures, the figures look shy,
Meanwhile the moon's hanging free in the sky.
Now tell me who's winning when the evening unfolds
And the city's playing brushes on a rhythm of gold?
I've mastered happy-hour water with exceptional flair,
Turn a free sample tray to a banquet affair.
And every time I'm told success is measured in stacks,
I remember all the wealthy folks too busy to relax.
So raise a cup—paper cup, chipped mug, whatever,
To the art of feeling good through imperfect weather.
My funds may be humble, but my spirits stay grand,
Got a shoestring budget and a big-band plan.

I'm broke, no joke, but I'm floating just fine,
Got a pocket full of rhythm and a head full of shine.
Ain't counting big money, I'm counting good days,
Little wins, big grins, in a hundred small ways.
I'm broke, no joke, but I'm rich where it counts,
Got friends at the table and a laugh that amounts
To more than a number in a ledger can say—
Got less in the bank, but more life in the day.

songs.subject: city from rooftop at dawn, orchestral-pop
songs.lyrics:
The night is folding velvet from the shoulders of the sky,
Streetlights flicker like they're learning how to say goodbye.
From up here every window is a breathing constellation,
Little worlds turning over in a quiet synchronization.
Coffee steam and taxi horns begin their soft reprise,
Elevators hum like secrets rising slowly through the rise.
And the river cuts a silver seam through sleeping steel and stone,
While the city stretches out its hands and starts to wake alone.

Oh, I’m watching it unfold like a symphony in light,
Every heartbeat of the buildings coming back to life.
From the rooftop we are standing where the sky meets memory,
And the dawn writes its confession on the glass of everything.
Let it rise, let it shine, let it spill across the air,
A thousand little stories waking up from everywhere.
In this moment we are endless, in this fragile, golden hour,
As the city sings itself into its morning power.

Billboards lose their neon like old dreams dissolve,
And the bakery below us starts its sugar-dusted calls.
The buses trace their routes like familiar melodies,
While pigeons choreograph the sky in lazy harmonies.
Somewhere a window opens, letting laughter slip outside,
And a violin of sirens tunes the edges of the sky.
Even shadows soften now, no longer sharp or cold,
As the dawn begins its painting in impossible gold.

Oh, I’m watching it unfold like a symphony in light,
Every heartbeat of the buildings coming back to life.
From the rooftop we are standing where the sky meets memory,
And the dawn writes its confession on the glass of everything.
Let it rise, let it shine, let it spill across the air,
A thousand little stories waking up from everywhere.
In this moment we are endless, in this fragile, golden hour,
As the city sings itself into its morning power.

Hold this silence like a promise on the edge of turning time,
Where the night still lingers softly in the echo of a chime.
We are small against the skyline, but for now we understand,
Every ending is a beginning drawn in sunlight’s hand.
And if we ever lose this feeling in the rush of daily pace,
We can climb back up in memory to this quiet, holy place.
Where the city, half-awakened, wore a halo made of air,
And for just a breath of living, everything was there.

songs.subject: sneaker flipping, trap rap
songs.lyrics:
Kickbox stock, I flip Nikes in the night plot, tight knot
Price drop? I buy all, supply crawl, I hijack the hype spot
Triple-step syllables, minimal miracles, residual digital
Flip pairs like flip-cards, wrist hard, profits get pivotal
Boxed up, I ship out, zip-lip, no slip-up, I skip doubt
Quick route from thrift towns to rich clouds, I lift clout
In the backroom, stacking tags, black bags, cash loop
Every outsole I touch turn plush—now that’s truth
Three stripes, I resell dreams in clean price extremes
Bean-count fiend, I scheme between seams and green beams
Deadstock to the desktop, nonstop, I let stock pop
Every checkout sound like a drum when the flex drops

Flip, flip, quick grip, I run the whole stockroom
Trip-trip, wrist hit, then I zoom past the cost boom
Buy low, sky high, every pair do the sky dive
I don’t walk—I resell motion in a trap-life pipeline

songs.subject: father's grave, folk
songs.lyrics:
I walked the lane where the hedges bow,
Past fields you taught me to plow,
The wind kept low, like it knew somehow
I wasn’t there to stay.
The gate was cold in my open hand,
The stones half-sunk in the land,
Your name still carved, still hard to stand
And say out loud that day.

And I sat with the silence you left behind,
Like a thread pulled loose through the fabric of time,
No grand goodbye, no warning sign,
Just the shape of you in the air.
I brought no words that could make it right,
Just the weight of the road and the fading light,
And I let the wind do what it might,
While I learned how to be there.

The grass had grown through the broken stone,
Like it wanted you less alone,
Like the earth itself had overthrown
The edges of your name.
I traced the letters with weathered thumb,
Thought of the things I should have become,
But all my answers came out numb
In the hush where I stood the same.

If I’d known the last time was the last time,
I’d have slowed every word, every line, every sign,
But memory moves like a river gone blind
To the hands that try to hold.
So I leave you here where the sky runs low,
Where the quiet things continue to grow,
And I carry the weight of the things I don’t know
Back down the road.
songs.lyrics_en:
I walked the lane where the hedges bow,
Past fields you taught me to plow,
The wind kept low, like it knew somehow
I wasn’t there to stay.
The gate was cold in my open hand,
The stones half-sunk in the land,
Your name still carved, still hard to stand
And say out loud that day.

And I sat with the silence you left behind,
Like a thread pulled loose through the fabric of time,
No grand goodbye, no warning sign,
Just the shape of you in the air.
I brought no words that could make it right,
Just the weight of the road and the fading light,
And I let the wind do what it might,
While I learned how to be there.

The grass had grown through the broken stone,
Like it wanted you less alone,
Like the earth itself had overthrown
The edges of your name.
I traced the letters with weathered thumb,
Thought of the things I should have become,
But all my answers came out numb
In the hush where I stood the same.

If I’d known the last time was the last time,
I’d have slowed every word, every line, every sign,
But memory moves like a river gone blind
To the hands that try to hold.
So I leave you here where the sky runs low,
Where the quiet things continue to grow,
And I carry the weight of the things I don’t know
Back down the road.

songs.subject: quitting job, ska
songs.lyrics:
Alarm clock screaming like a broken siren,
Same gray cubicle, same soul defyin’,
Boss man pacing with a clipboard grin,
Counting every second I’m trapped in.
Printer jams like it knows my pain,
Coffee tastes like a paper chain,
Clock hands laughing as they crawl so slow,
But today I’m ready—I’m about to go.

I quit, I quit, watch me kick that door,
No more 9-to-5 dragging me to the floor,
Skankin’ down the street like I own the sky,
Got my two weeks notice and a brand new high.
I quit, I quit, hear the brass line sing,
Freedom hits harder than a punk downbeat swing,
Turn it up, let the good life spin,
I’m out that door and I’m never back in.

Stapler clicking like a metronome curse,
Every meeting feels like it could get worse,
Email overload, I’m buried in files,
But I’m mentally skankin’ for miles and miles.
Clock hits five and I don’t hesitate,
Drop that resignation like a clean backbeat break,
Boss says “wait,” but I’m already gone,
Got my sneakers on and I’m moving on.

Now the street’s got a rhythm I can feel in my chest,
Every step I take is a bassline blessed,
No more chains, no more fluorescent skies,
Just horns in my head and the world open wide.

songs.subject: endless summer, shoegaze
songs.lyrics:
Heat shimmer halo on the pavement light,
We melted into afternoons that wouldn’t end at night,
Your voice in slow-motion through a humming fan,
Time stretched thin like a sleeping band.
Windows open, dust in gold parade,
We traced the sky in the lemonade shade,
Every second felt like it forgot its name,
Like nothing before or after came.

And it felt endless, like a wave that never breaks,
Soft and weightless, like the air a memory makes,
We were drifting in a glow that wouldn’t fade,
In a summer that the silence never gave away.
Endless, in the blur of sun and skin,
Where the days didn’t start and the nights didn’t end,
Just a hum, just a haze, just you and me,
Lost in the shimmer of what used to be.

Radio static in a half-lit room,
Flowers leaning heavy in the afternoon,
We talked in circles just to hear the sound,
Of words dissolving as they hit the ground.
The world outside was a watercolor dream,
Edges unraveling at the seams,
And every goodbye we never said,
Still echoes soft in the air instead.

Now I chase that light through a muted sky,
Where the colors still refuse to die,
And I swear sometimes in the cooling breeze,
That summer breathes back through the trees.

songs.subject: dying town, punk
songs.lyrics:
Boarded-up windows like teeth knocked out,
Main Street’s a ghost with its lights all out,
Factory bones in a rusted chain,
Breathing smoke where the hope once came.
Church bells ring but no one shows,
Grass cracks through the sidewalks slow,
We learned to laugh at the sinking sound,
Of a town that’s folding itself down.

But we’re still here, fists in the air,
Screaming loud like we still care,
You can burn the signs, you can shut the doors,
But you can’t erase what we’re fighting for.
This town may die, but we won’t go quiet,
We spit in the face of the slow decline riot,
If the lights go out, we’ll spark the flame,
And shout our names in the ashes again.

Empty diner with a flickering neon hum,
Old jukebox stuck on a song gone numb,
Kids on corners with nowhere to run,
Counting down days like a loaded gun.
Mayor smiles with a suitcase plan,
Selling off land to the highest hand,
But we paint our rage on the crumbling walls,
And we echo back when the silence calls.

So let it rot, let it break, let it fall apart,
We’ll carve out a beat with a broken heart,
And when the last streetlight finally dies,
We’ll be the noise that survives.

songs.subject: slow sunday in love, neo-soul
songs.lyrics:
Sunlight slipping through the blinds like honey,
Your hand on mine, everything feels sunny,
Coffee steam drawing soft little curls,
In this quiet corner of the world.
No rush, no need to be anywhere,
Just your breath tangled up in the air,
Records spin slow like a memory,
Of how easy love can be.

It’s a slow Sunday morning in your arms,
No alarms, just the warmth of your calm,
We’re floating where the daylight leans in,
Letting time forget where it’s been.
Every second feels like a soft refrain,
Like a song that never needs to change,
Just you and me in this gentle glow,
Moving as slow as the love we know.

Window open, breeze in the kitchen light,
You laugh like everything’s alright,
Bare feet dancing on the wooden floor,
I don’t need anything more.
Your voice is a low and steady groove,
Pulling me into your easy mood,
And the world outside can wait its turn,
While in here we let it burn slow.

If all the noise ever comes back again,
We’ll find this moment and start it then,
Right here where the morning feels like home,
And we’re never alone.

songs.subject: park chess hustler, boom-bap
songs.lyrics:
Park bench prophet, hoodie up, thoughts in a foggy optic,
Board on the table, he stable but barely got logic,
Sixteen, seen kings fall to his pawn shop tactics,
Knights get boxed quick, bishops get lost in the static.
Streetlight chess clock, no tick-tock, just talk slick,
Old heads step up, he dust ’em with a quick trick,
“Kid, you ain’t ready,” they said with a grin thin,
Next thing they know, he’s checkmating in ten pins.
He learned patience from silence, violence in timing,
Every move like a line in a jazz-band rhyming,
Queens get tempted, then sent to their endgame,
He plays like Miles—no rush, just flame.
Backpack full of paperbacks, diagrams, faded maps,
Studying gambits while other kids chase fading caps,
But he chasing titles in parks with a cracked board,
Where every loss is a lesson, every win is a fact stored.

In the park where the nights get deep,
He’s playing kings while the city won’t sleep,
One move, two moves, three moves gone,
Turned sidewalk stones into a chessboard song.
They came for a game, but they left with doubt,
’Cause the kid in the hoodie just figured it out,
From pawn to power in the lamplight glow,
That’s how a hustler’s legend starts to grow.

songs.subject: quiet marriage, country
songs.lyrics:
The porch light hums like it’s tired too,
Same two rocking chairs, same worn-out view,
We pass like smoke in a still-lit room,
Too careful not to collide.
Your coffee’s cold by the morning mail,
My words fall short like a half-told tale,
We’ve learned every silence too well,
And neither of us asks why.

It’s a quiet kind of breaking, slow and sure,
Like a house that forgets what it was built for,
We still share the bed but not the night,
Two shadows just doing time.
No storms left here, just fading sound,
Of two hearts that don’t talk out loud,
And I can’t tell if we’re still in love,
Or just afraid to let it go.

The radio plays what we used to sing,
But neither of us hums anything,
Your hand on mine feels like a ring
That’s lost its shape and shine.
We count the years in dishes and bills,
In the way the kitchen never feels still,
Like something waiting for a will
Neither of us can sign.

If I said your name like it used to be,
Would it echo back or just disappear to sea,
In the space between you and me,
Where the truth won’t come to stay?

songs.subject: surviving hard year, arena-rock
songs.lyrics:
I wore the weight of a hundred storms,
Walked through fire in borrowed forms,
Every sunrise felt like a test I’d fail,
Every night like a setting sail.
I lost my footing, I lost my name,
Got cut by doubt but I stayed in the game,
Bit down hard on the edge of fear,
But I made it through—I’m still here.

And I’m standing in the wreckage singing loud,
Heart like thunder, head above the crowd,
Yeah, I broke but I rebuilt every piece,
Turned my scars into release.
This is my battle cry in the light of the flame,
I survived every shadow that called my name,
From the ashes I rise, unchained and free,
Nothing left that can bury me.

I learned the sound of my own grit,
When every loss tried to make me quit,
But I hit back harder with every fall,
Started climbing over every wall.
The mirrors cracked but I looked right in,
Saw the fight underneath my skin,
And I swore to the ghost of who I’d been,
I would not bow again.

So let the sky come crashing down,
I’ve already learned to stand my ground,
And if the world tries to pull me under,
I’ll come back louder, I’ll come back thunder.

songs.subject: dancing badly, swing
songs.lyrics:
I step out sharp in my two-left shoes,
Tryin’ to catch that rhythm and the swingin’ blues,
Band kicks off and I lose the beat,
My feet got plans they won’t repeat.
I spin too soon, I lean too late,
Still grinnin’ wide like it’s all first-rate,
Couples glide like they own the floor,
I’m just chaos in a vintage score.

But I’m dancin’ bad, and I’m feelin’ good,
Movin’ like I probably never should,
I trip, I twist, I laugh out loud,
Spinnin’ myself right into the crowd.
Ain’t got grace but I got this joy,
Shufflin’ round like a wind-up toy,
If this is wrong then I don’t care,
I’m swingin’ anyway in the Saturday air.

My hat flies off on a careless turn,
But I keep goin’ like I never learn,
Band hits a break and I miss the cue,
So I improvise like I’m s’posed to do.
Folks all watch with a puzzled grin,
But I bow like I meant that spin,
Floorboards creak to my clumsy beat,
And I make it work with my off-time feet.

Let ‘em dance clean, let ‘em dance tight,
I’m out here shakin’ up the night,
Every stumble just part of the show,
And I’m exactly where I wanna go.

songs.subject: town under reservoir, indie
songs.lyrics:
They raised the water where the church bells rang,
Where the old school yard still remembered your name,
Now the map just blurs into blue and gray,
And the road signs rust underwater decay.
I walk the ridge where the lookout stood,
Where your laughter once split the neighborhood,
But the valley’s gone, just a glassy sheet,
Hiding everything beneath.

And I swear I can still hear it below,
In the quiet undertow,
A town that’s breathing under the tide,
Where all our memories hide.
It’s sleeping deep where the current turns,
In the places that no light returns,
And I come back just to feel it again,
Like a ghost in the rain.

They said it was progress, said it was fate,
That the river needed a wider gate,
But I see rooftops when the wind is right,
Just beneath the shimmer at night.
Every ripple feels like a name I knew,
Every wave like a voice breaking through,
Calling out from a drowned-out past,
That refuses to pass.

If I whisper down to where you are,
Does it travel like a fallen star,
Through the water dark and wide and deep,
Where the vanished city sleeps?

songs.subject: studying at 2am, lo-fi
songs.lyrics:
2 a.m. glow on the cracked laptop screen,
Highlighter bleeding through the margin between,
Empty coffee cup, cold like my focus,
Thoughts drift off but I try to notice.
Pages stacking like unfinished dreams,
Time moving slower than it ever seems,
Rain on the window in a steady loop,
I replay mistakes in a tired group.
Headphones on but the silence creeps,
Even the bassline hums when the room won’t sleep,
I underline words I won’t remember,
Lost in the quiet of this long November.

And I’m studying alone at 2 a.m.,
Chasing daylight I can’t reach again,
Every problem feels like it knows my name,
Every answer looks the same.
Lo-fi heartbeat in a half-lit haze,
Trying to find my way through these days,
Just me and the page and the drifting sound,
In a world that’s slowing down.

songs.subject: unrequited love, mariachi
songs.lyrics:
I brought you roses wrapped in firelight,
Serenata under your window at night,
The trumpet cried what I could not say,
As you turned your face away.
The guitar trembled in my shaking hand,
Like it understood what I couldn’t stand,
That your heart was a closed-up door,
And I was knocking forevermore.

Ay, mi corazón, it beats for you alone,
Like a thunder in a hollow stone,
But you don’t hear the rhythm I bleed,
You don’t see how much I need.
I sing so loud the stars all shake,
Still you don’t feel the love I make,
Unrequited, I remain,
A love song lost in pouring rain.

The plaza echoes with my broken tune,
Beneath the watchful eye of a silver moon,
I pour my soul into every chord,
Still you walk like I’m ignored.
The brass explodes like a wounded sky,
Each note a question of “why, oh why,”
But you dance in another’s light,
While I dissolve into the night.

If I could trade this pain for dust,
I would scatter it, I must,
But even dust would spell your name,
Burning soft within the flame.

songs.subject: wordless couple, tender
songs.lyrics:
You hum in the kitchen, I know what it means,
It’s the soft kind of language between the routines,
A glance over coffee, a tilt of your head,
Says more than the words we never said.
We move like a rhythm that nobody hears,
Built out of silence over the years,
Even the pauses feel like home,
When we’re not alone.

We don’t need words, we don’t need sound,
To know what the other is thinking about,
It’s in the space where the quiet lives,
In the way your hand just fits.
Every look is a sentence I read,
Every breath is all I need,
We speak in the language of simply being,
You and me, understanding.

You turn the light down before I ask,
I already know what the moment asks,
We share the air like it’s second skin,
Letting the stillness in.
A thousand thoughts go unspoken here,
But nothing between us disappears,
We just exist in the same soft frame,
No need to name.

If the world went quiet and lost its voice,
We’d still know how to make the choice,
To find each other without a trace,
Just the shape of space.
songs.lyrics_en:
You hum in the kitchen, I know what it means,
It’s the soft kind of language between the routines,
A glance over coffee, a tilt of your head,
Says more than the words we never said.
We move like a rhythm that nobody hears,
Built out of silence over the years,
Even the pauses feel like home,
When we’re not alone.

We don’t need words, we don’t need sound,
To know what the other is thinking about,
It’s in the space where the quiet lives,
In the way your hand just fits.
Every look is a sentence I read,
Every breath is all I need,
We speak in the language of simply being,
You and me, understanding.

You turn the light down before I ask,
I already know what the moment asks,
We share the air like it’s second skin,
Letting the stillness in.
A thousand thoughts go unspoken here,
But nothing between us disappears,
We just exist in the same soft frame,
No need to name.

If the world went quiet and lost its voice,
We’d still know how to make the choice,
To find each other without a trace,
Just the shape of space.

songs.subject: factory closing, blues
songs.lyrics:
The whistle don’t blow like it used to do,
Just a hollow wind through the smokestack blue,
Lockers still rattlin’ in the empty hall,
But nobody answers the breakroom call.
Grease on the floor where the good times stood,
Machines gone cold in the ironwood,
Foreman reads it off a folded page,
“Shut it down for the modern age.”

And it’s goodbye, goodbye to the factory line,
Where we traded our hours for borrowed time,
Steel don’t cry but we sure do,
When the paycheck fades from view.
We built this town with a worn-out hand,
Now it’s sinking in the shifting sand,
One by one as the doors unwind,
It’s the end of the assembly line.

Old man Jenkins stares at the gate,
Says he knew this was comin’, just a matter of fate,
Lunch pails rustin’ in a busted rack,
No more shift to bring ’em back.
The clock still ticks but it don’t mean a thing,
No more punch cards, no more ring,
Just echoes driftin’ through the grime,
Of another slower crime.

If the furnace cools and the lights go black,
Ain’t no easy way to bring it back,
But we’ll remember in the dust and the haze,
The sound of our working days.

songs.subject: last night festival, house
songs.lyrics:
Neon sky fading into gold and flame,
Bass still shaking every voice, every name,
Shoes in the dust where the hours ran free,
We were everything we were meant to be.
Hands in the air like we’re holding the stars,
Lost in the echo of synth guitars,
Last set playing like it can’t be real,
But we feel what we feel what we feel.

This is the last night, don’t let it fade,
We’re dancing in the light that the memories made,
Heartbeat syncing with the four-on-floor,
We don’t want it to end anymore.
Raise it higher, let the moment ignite,
We are infinite in the flashing light,
One more drop and we disappear,
Together right here.

Confetti falling like a summer rain,
Every stranger knows your face by name,
We sing along with the fading sun,
Like the night has only just begun.
Speakers crying out their final hymn,
And we’re all glowing at the edge of the rim,
Of a moment that we can’t define,
But it’s yours and mine.

When the silence comes and the lights go down,
We’ll still hear that electric sound,
In every step when we leave this place,
It’ll echo in space.

songs.subject: procrastination diss, rap
songs.lyrics:
Yo, I’m beefin’ with myself, that lazy internal CEO,
Sleepin’ on my dreams like they rent-free in escrow,
“Start tomorrow,” yeah, that’s my favorite lie,
Got more “later” plans than stars in the sky.
I ghost my goals like they texted too soon,
Then blame Mercury, the clock, and the moon,
My focus got commitment issues, it flees,
I’m the only one sabotaging me with ease.
I sit down to work, then I “quickly” redecorate,
Watch videos on productivity I’ll never emulate,
My to-do list is a myth I keep extendin’,
Plot twist: I’m the villain I keep defending.

Pro-cras-ti-nation, you my worst opp,
Got me saying “five minutes” till the clock stop,
We been locked in a battle, no clear solution,
I keep losing to my own confusion.
Yeah, I roast myself but I never improve,
Diss track on me but I still don’t move,
One day I’ll win, but not today—
I’ll start this song… okay, okay, okay.
