songs.subject: a funk song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Dust on my boots, and a thousand-yard stare
Train whistle crying like it knows what I bear
Pocket full of letters I never replied
Now I’m counting streetlights as I ride back inside

Uniform heavy but it don’t feel like mine
Like I borrowed a body from a different timeline
Every mile marker’s a ghost in my mind
But I’m closer to home with each click of the line

I’m coming home, coming home, let the rhythm take me down
Back to the bassline heartbeat of this sleeping little town
I’ve been far, I’ve been gone, but the night is letting go
And I’m stepping off the war and into funk I used to know
Yeah I’m coming home, coming home, let the horns call out my name
Where the hurt turns into music and the silence turns to flame
I’ve been gone too long to stay a shadow in the chrome
So I’m riding that groove all the way back home

Mama on the porch with a lantern in her hand
Like she’s been holding time the best she can stand
And I don’t know what version of me she will see
But she smiles like she still believes in me

There’s a crack in the sidewalk where I left my youth
Now I’m walking on truth that don’t feel like truth
Every step is a drumbeat, every breath is a song
But I gotta learn where my heart still belongs

I’m coming home, coming home, let the rhythm take me down
Back to the bassline heartbeat of this sleeping little town
I’ve been far, I’ve been gone, but the night is letting go
And I’m stepping off the war and into funk I used to know
Yeah I’m coming home, coming home, let the horns call out my name
Where the hurt turns into music and the silence turns to flame
I’ve been gone too long to stay a shadow in the chrome
So I’m riding that groove all the way back home

And the silence hits different when the guns ain’t in your ear
It’s loud in a way that makes you feel everything you fear
But the band keeps playing like a prayer through the dark
Turning every broken memory into a spark

I’m coming home, coming home, let the rhythm take me down
Back to the bassline heartbeat of this sleeping little town
I’ve been far, I’ve been gone, but the night is letting go
And I’m stepping off the war and into funk I used to know
Yeah I’m coming home, coming home, let the horns call out my name
Where the hurt turns into music and the silence turns to flame
I’ve been gone too long to stay a shadow in the chrome
So I’m riding that groove all the way back home

songs.subject: a swing song about falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I caught your laugh on a Sunday street,
Where the bakery air and the church bells meet,
Same old corners, new heartbeat,
When you turned around and looked at me.
Dust on the window of the corner store,
Where we swore we’d never want nothing more,
Now every step feels like a dance floor,
Since I saw you like never before.

Oh, I’m falling like a record spinning out of time,
Like a brass band swinging on a midnight line,
In the town where we grew up, yours and mine,
Now every little memory’s a valentine.
And I can’t pretend I don’t feel this way,
Like the hometown lights just learned to play,
Oh, I’m falling, falling in a swing parade,
For you in the place we were both made.

We know the cracks in every sidewalk stone,
Every shortcut path we ever roamed,
But I never knew I’d feel at home,
In the way your eyes made the ordinary glow.
Now the streetlights hum a different tune,
Like they’re nodding when I walk past you,
Even the wind seems to be in on it too,
Whispering secrets I never knew.

Oh, I’m falling like a record spinning out of time,
Like a brass band swinging on a midnight line,
In the town where we grew up, yours and mine,
Now every little memory’s a valentine.
And I can’t pretend I don’t feel this way,

songs.subject: a post-rock song about a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Her hands were maps of winter light
Folded rivers in the morning white
I learned the world between her lines
Soft as dust in borrowed time
She held the kettle like a prayer
Steam wrote stories in the air
Every wrinkle, every seam
A place I once believed in

Oh, her hands, they carried whole seasons
Breaking bread with silent reasons
They knew the weight of letting go
And how to make the broken whole
Oh, her hands, they never trembled
Even when the years disassembled
Everything we thought would stay
They just kept shaping yesterday

She brushed the years from my sleeve
Like fallen leaves no one could grieve
And when she laughed, the room would bend
As if time forgot to end
There was a hymn in every touch
That said, “You do not need so much”
And I would listen, I would learn
While distant photographs would burn

Oh, her hands, they carried whole seasons
Breaking bread with silent reasons
They knew the weight of letting go
And how to make the broken whole
Oh, her hands, they never trembled
Even when the years disassembled
Everything we thought would stay
They just kept shaping yesterday

Now silence sits where warmth once grew
A chair that still remembers you
And I trace ghosts across the air
As if your fingers still are there
If I could speak the way you spoke
In touch, in light, in steady hope
I’d let the world fall from my grip
And learn again your fingertips

songs.subject: a swing song about texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The clock ticks loud in my dim-lit room,
Got a glass of gin and a hint of doom,
Your name pops up like a loaded dice,
I told myself “don’t do this twice.”
But my thumbs start dancing on the screen,
Typing words I didn’t mean,
A little “hey” turns into flame,
And I swear I’m not the one to blame.

I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be,
Like a trumpet call in a smoky sea,
It swings and sways, it won’t let go,
That late-night rhythm saying “go, go, go.”
I know it’s wrong but it feels so right,
Like a jazz band playing through the night,
I’m caught in a melody wild and free—
I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be.

Your replies come fast, a syncopated beat,
Like tap shoes knocking down my feet,
We trade old jokes and borrowed sins,
And the whole night bends and spins.
The streetlights hum like a brassy horn,
I should’ve quit before it was born,
But every “lol” and every sign,
Pulls me deeper every line.

I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be,
Like a saxophone crying out at sea,
It swings too hard, it bends too low,
And I can’t find the way to say “no, no, no.”
I know it’s wrong but it feels so right,
Like a crowded club on a Friday night,
I’m lost in a rhythm I can’t unsee—
I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be.

Now the silence waits on the edge of dawn,
And I know I should’ve just left it alone,
But the last “you up?” still glows on my screen,
Like a spotlight cutting through a dream.
If I step away, will it fade away?
Or come back louder another day?
This swinging heart won’t sit still for me—
It keeps on texting what it shouldn’t be.

songs.subject: a ska song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Same cracked pavement, same chain-link sky
Same corner store where the hours go by
Streetlight flickers like it knows my name
Everybody here stuck in the same old game
But I got rhythm in my worn-out shoes
A one-way ticket and too much to lose
I hear a horn line cutting through the gray
Saying “move along, there’s a better way”

We’re breaking out of this dead-end block
Where the same four walls are all we’ve got
Step by step when the bassline drops
We don’t stop, we don’t stop, we don’t stop
From the backstreets to the open sun
We’re the ones who refuse to be done
Hear the skank of a brand new shock
We’re breaking out of this dead-end block

Bus stop dreams on a faded bench
Writing escape plans in a busted-up sketch
Friends all saying “that’s just talk”
But I’ve been counting every ticking clock
Found a downbeat in a radio song
Telling me I was never wrong
So I pack my doubts in a paper bag
And I follow the snare like a beating flag

We’re breaking out of this dead-end block
Where the same four walls are all we’ve got
Step by step when the bassline drops
We don’t stop, we don’t stop, we don’t stop
From the backstreets to the open sun
We’re the ones who refuse to be done
Hear the skank of a brand new shock
We’re breaking out of this dead-end block

And if the night tries to pull me under
I’ll turn it up like brass and thunder
Every setback is just a start
Every “no” is a spark in my heart
So I swing through the gates of doubt
Let the old life fade out, fade out

We’re breaking out of this dead-end block
Where the same four walls are all we’ve got
Step by step when the bassline drops
We don’t stop, we don’t stop, we don’t stop
From the backstreets to the open sun
We’re the ones who refuse to be done
Hear the skank of a brand new shock
We’re breaking out of this dead-end block

songs.subject: a klezmer song about texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
In the glow of my phone at three in the night
The city’s asleep but my thumb is alight
I scroll through the names like a half-spun prayer
And stop at the one I should never dare
Your name in my screen like a mischievous tune
Like a fiddle that laughs at a wandering moon
I promise myself “no, not again”
Then I write “hey” and I press send

Oy, I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be texting
My heart is a bandoneon flexing and vexing
Every buzz is a drum, every read is a sting
And my better intentions just fly on a string
Oy, I’m laughing and crying, confused and obsessed
With a message I sent when I should have said “rest”
Now the night plays a klezmer inside of my chest
And I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be texting

The light from the screen paints my face pale blue
Like a candlelit tale that I can’t undo
You reply with a winking, half-hearted hello
And I’m already lost in the afterglow
The words start to dance like a drunken parade
Like clarinets weaving through choices I made
I type and I delete and I type once more
Like footsteps returning to a forbidden door

Oy, I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be texting
My judgment is slipping, my conscience is resting
Every vibration’s a slap and a kiss
In a carnival spun out of digital bliss
Oy, I’m laughing and crying, I’m lost in between
What I know that is wise and what flashes the screen
And the night keeps on playing that old violin scene
While I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be texting

If I put down the phone, will the silence grow loud?
Will I finally stand still, or dissolve in the crowd?
There’s a ghost in the glow and it won’t let me go
Like a tune from a street where I used to know
But the fiddle still calls through the wires and the glass
Saying “come, just one more, let the boundaries pass”
And I answer in echoes I cannot control
With a trembling thumb and a wandering soul

Oy, I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be texting
My heart is a question I keep on re-asking
Every word is a spin of the wheel and the flame
In a game where I never quite learn the name
Oy, I’m laughing and crying, I’m tangled, confessed
To a melody wrapped in my ribcage’s nest
And the night plays along like it knows all the rest
While I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be texting

songs.subject: a jazz song about looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
The sign-up sheet was coffee-stained and shaking in my hand
My name looked like a question I was trying to understand
The room was half-lit laughter, smoke curling in the air
And every empty table felt like someone judging there
I tuned my voice to courage, but it kept slipping out of key
My heart was a brushed snare drum rattling inside me
The host called out the numbers, like a doorbell to my fate
And I told myself “just breathe,” though I was already too late

And I remember that first open mic
Where the silence felt louder than the light
Every note a little stumble, every word a little tight
Still I sang like it could save my life
Oh I didn’t know what I didn’t know
But I let my trembling voice still go
And somehow in that fragile, falling flight
I became myself that night

The stage was barely bigger than the doubts beneath my shoes
The mic was like a mirror showing everything to lose
I laughed at my own timing, I forgot the second line
But the band just kept on walking like they’d known me all the time
There was a woman by the counter tapping rhythms with her glass
An old man closed his eyes like he was hearing his own past
And for a moment I was floating, not so terrified to fail
Just a melody of nerves in a slightly crooked scale

And I remember that first open mic
Where the silence felt louder than the light
Every note a little stumble, every word a little tight
Still I sang like it could save my life
Oh I didn’t know what I didn’t know
But I let my trembling voice still go
And somehow in that fragile, falling flight
I became myself that night

Now I walk into the room and I recognize that air
The same old wooden stage, the same uncertain prayer
But something in me softened where there used to be a fight
Like I learned to trust the dark between the notes and light

And I remember that first open mic
Like a photograph I hold inside
Every fear I thought would break me somehow turned out to be right
To bring me to the other side
Oh I didn’t know what I didn’t know
But I let my trembling voice still go
And in every room where strangers gather, still I find that night
I’m back at my first open mic

songs.subject: a jazz song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone light flickers in a quiet kitchen glow
Dust on the memories I never let you know
Your name sits heavy like a record on the shelf
Spinning in silence I’ve been keeping to myself
I walk the same streets where I learned to disappear
Every step an echo of the things I didn’t hear
Years like smoke between the truth and what I said
Now I’m dialing numbers that I swore were long dead

Ma, can you hear me through the static and the years?
It’s your child on the line with a voice full of tears
I don’t have the words but I’ve come so far to try
If you’re still out there, pick up tonight

The jazz bar’s humming like a secret I once knew
A trumpet bends the light the way regret can do
Every note is asking what I couldn’t understand
How love can slip away like sand through trembling hands
I practiced all my sentences, then threw them out again
Nothing sounds like truth when it’s been sharpened into pain
So I let the silence do the talking I can’t say
Hoping you still recognize the child who ran away

Ma, can you hear me through the static and the years?
It’s your child on the line with a voice full of tears
I don’t have the words but I’ve come so far to try
If you’re still out there, pick up tonight

And if the line goes empty, I’ll still leave my name
Like a candle in the window of a house that’s not the same
Forgiveness isn’t instant, it’s a slow and tender rain
Falling on the distance between love and all its pain
But I heard in every melody that drifts through smoky air
That even broken things can learn to say a prayer

Ma, can you hear me through the static and the years?
It’s your child on the line with a voice full of tears
I don’t have the words but I’ve come so far to try
If you’re still out there, pick up tonight

Mama workin’ double shifts, still barely makin’ rent
I was dodgin’ every siren like it’s heaven-sent

songs.subject: a hip hop song about the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The room still hums where your words used to land
Like broken glass memories slipping through my hand
Clock ticks loud but we don’t say a thing
Just two silhouettes in the dust of what we bring
You on the couch, I’m pacing the floor
Both of us hurt but we don’t know what for
Echoes of anger still hanging in air
We act like we’re fine but we’re barely there
I replay the moment, every line we threw
Sharper than we meant, now cutting us through
You look at your phone like it’s safer to see
Than the face of the one who you used to believe

It’s the quiet after the fire burns out
Where love gets loud in a different kind of doubt
No doors slam, no more words to defend
Just the silence we don’t know how to mend
And I’m right here but it feels like a mile
Between your eyes and the ghost of your smile
We don’t break, we just don’t know how to stay
In the quiet after we push love away

You sigh like a question you won’t let me answer
Like hope got tired and lost its enhancer
I wanna reach out, but pride’s in the way
So we both stay stuck in the words we won’t say
Kitchen light flickers like it knows the mood
Cold coffee sitting where we used to be good
I hear your breath in the space between us
Heavy with truth neither one of us trusts
We built this home out of laughter and fire
Now every step feels like walking a wire
I miss how your voice used to call me back in
Now it just echoes where we don’t begin

It’s the quiet after the fire burns out
Where love gets loud in a different kind of doubt
No doors slam, no more words to defend
Just the silence we don’t know how to mend
And I’m right here but it feels like a mile
Between your eyes and the ghost of your smile
We don’t break, we just don’t know how to stay
In the quiet after we push love away

If silence could talk it would scream what we mean
That we’re both still here but stuck in between
The hurt and the healing, the now and the then
Wondering if we can start over again
I don’t need perfect, just need you to see
That the worst of this fight isn’t you or me
It’s the space that we let grow cold and wide
When neither of us wants to step to the other side

It’s the quiet after the fire burns out
Where love gets loud in a different kind of doubt
No doors slam, no more words to defend
Just the silence we don’t know how to mend
And I’m right here but it feels like a mile
Between your eyes and the ghost of your smile
We don’t break, we just don’t know how to stay
In the quiet after we push love away

songs.subject: a ska song about working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Alarm clock screams at 5:09
Coffee’s cold but I say I’m fine
Punch the first clock, then I punch the next
Running on empty and over-stressed
Bus ride dreams of a better day
But the rent still laughs, it won’t go away
Check my pockets, just lint and doubt
But I keep moving, I can’t tap out

I’m working two jobs and still falling behind
Bills keep stacking in the back of my mind
Every paycheck’s gone before it arrives
Tell me how I’m supposed to survive
I’m spinning wheels but I’m stuck in place
Running this race at a losing pace
Working two jobs and still falling behind
Still falling behind

Boss says “smile,” I say “yes, sir”
Clock ticks louder than the blender’s blur
Second shift lights feel just like day
But my energy’s fading away
Try to save but it disappears
Broken hopes and unpaid years
Every dollar feels already spent
On promises I never meant

I’m working two jobs and still falling behind
Bills keep stacking in the back of my mind
Every paycheck’s gone before it arrives
Tell me how I’m supposed to survive
I’m spinning wheels but I’m stuck in place
Running this race at a losing pace
Working two jobs and still falling behind
Still falling behind

If I keep going, where does it end?
Just more hours I can’t defend
I need a break but I don’t know how
The future’s always “later” but I need it now
So I laugh it off, but it cuts so deep
Dreams I’m too tired to even keep

I’m working two jobs and still falling behind
Bills keep stacking in the back of my mind
Every paycheck’s gone before it arrives
Tell me how I’m supposed to survive
I’m spinning wheels but I’m stuck in place
Running this race at a losing pace
Working two jobs and still falling behind
Still falling behind
